full_prompt: Design a single-part monolithic Experiment Attachment System (EAS) bracket per the NASA / open-CAD platform EAS Challenge. Purpose: provide a standardized rapid-install attachment point for crew-accessible experiments inside the ISS, interfacing to the MS33601 standard ISS seat-track rails. Geometric constraints: bracket mates to MS33601 seat track (20 mm slot, 0.5 in wide x 1.0 in deep) on one side; on the other side provides a planar mounting face with 4x M6 tapped holes on a 76 mm x 50 mm rectangular pattern for crew experiments. Overall bounding box 180 mm x 80 mm x 75 mm. The bracket must include defined keep-out zones (per challenge brief): no part of the bracket may protrude into the MS33601 seat-track slot envelope except the interface lug. Material: 7075-T73 aluminum (E = 71.7 GPa, yield = 435 MPa, ultimate = 505 MPa, density = 2810 kg/m^3). Load cases: (R1) 1000 N (approx 225 lbf) applied at the experiment-mount face in the pull-out direction (away from seat track); max von Mises <= yield / 2.0 = 217 MPa; deflection at load point <= 0.5 in (12.7 mm). (R2) 500 N applied laterally (parallel to seat-track rail) at the experiment-mount face; max von Mises <= 217 MPa; deflection <= 12.7 mm. (R3) 300 N moment-arm pull: 300 N applied 50 mm off-axis producing bending moment 15 N*m at the seat-track interface; max von Mises <= 217 MPa. (R4) Bracket mass <= 20 lb (9.07 kg) - this is the challenge hard cap but trivially met (aim for <= 500 g for realistic scoring). (R5) Linear buckling eigenvalue under LC1 compressive reaction path >= 3.0. (R6) Modal first frequency hard-mounted at MS33601 interface >= 50 Hz (ISS mission requirement). Primary verification: structural FEA (linear static x3) + modal + buckling. Vibration secondary via R6. Thermal, fluid, radiation NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 + Parasolid monolithic bracket, MS33601 interface drawing, and a report listing mass in g, max von Mises per case in MPa, deflection at load per case in mm, first buckling load factor, first modal frequency in Hz.
short_prompt:
Design a single-part monolithic Experiment Attachment System (EAS) bracket per the NASA open-CAD EAS Challenge. The bracket provides a standardized rapid-install attachment inside the ISS between the MS33601 seat-track rails on one side and a planar 4 x M6 experiment-mount face (76 x 50 mm pattern) on the other. Keep-out: no part of the bracket may protrude into the MS33601 slot envelope except the interface lug. Material 7075-T73 aluminum.

Envelope: bounding 180 x 80 x 75 mm.

Load cases.
- LC1 pull-out: 1000 N (~225 lbf) at the experiment-mount face away from the seat track; load-point deflection <= 12.7 mm (0.5 in).
- LC2 lateral: 500 N parallel to the seat-track rail at the experiment-mount face; deflection <= 12.7 mm.
- LC3 moment-arm pull: 300 N applied 50 mm off-axis = 15 N*m bending at the seat-track interface.
- LC4 linear buckling under LC1 compressive reaction path, eigenvalue >= 3.0.
- LC5 modal: hard-mounted at the MS33601 interface, first frequency >= 50 Hz (ISS mission requirement).

prompt.title: NASA EAS seat-track experiment bracket
prompt.brief: 7075-T73 monolithic bracket on MS33601 ISS seat-track. 1000/500/300 N load cases + 50 Hz modal floor.
